hypothesis: If diffusion-based generative models (CDVAE, GemsDiff, DiffCSP) are trained on the preprocessed MP and OQMD datasets, then they will produce crystal structures that are more chemically valid and thermodynamically stable—exhibiting lower RMSD after DFT relaxation, lower energy above hull (higher SUN scores), and closer property and latent-space distributions to the training data—than controlled perturbations of known materials, because the models learn underlying physical correlations rather than introducing random distortions.
